source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That Separation of Duties Is Enforced While Assigning KMS Related Roles to Users

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That Separation of Duties Is Enforced While Assigning KMS Related Roles to Users
input_row.description: It is recommended that the principle of 'Separation of Duties' is enforced while assigning KMS related roles to users.
input_row.rationale: The built-in/predefined IAM role Cloud KMS Admin allows the user/identity to create, delete, and manage service account(s). The built-in/predefined IAM role Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter/Decrypter allows the user/identity (with adequate privileges on concerned resources) to encrypt and decrypt data at rest using an encryption key(s). The built-in/predefined IAM role Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter allows the user/identity (with adequate privileges on concerned resources) to encrypt data at rest using an encryption key(s). The built-in/predefined IAM role Cloud KMS CryptoKey Decrypter allows the user/identity (with adequate privileges on concerned resources) to decrypt data at rest using an encryption key(s). Separation of duties is the concept of ensuring that one individual does not have all necessary permissions to be able to complete a malicious action. In Cloud KMS, this could be an action such as using a key to access and decrypt data a user should not normally have access to. Separation of duties is a business control typically used in larger organizations, meant to help avoid security or privacy incidents and errors. It is considered best practice. No user(s) should have Cloud KMS Admin and any of the Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter/Decrypter, Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter, Cloud KMS CryptoKey Decrypter roles assigned at the same time. Impact: Removed roles should be assigned to another user based on business needs.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to IAM & Admin/IAM by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/iam 2. Ensure no member has the roles Cloud KMS Admin and any of the Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter/Decrypter, Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter, Cloud KMS CryptoKey Decrypter assigned. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all users and role assignments: gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID 2. Ensure that there are no common users found in the member section for roles cloudkms.admin and any one of Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter/Decrypter, Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter, Cloud KMS CryptoKey Decrypter
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to IAM & Admin/IAM using https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/iam 2. For any member having Cloud KMS Admin and any of the Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter/Decrypter, Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter, Cloud KMS CryptoKey Decrypter roles granted/assigned, click the Delete Bin icon to remove the role from the member. Note: Removing a role should be done based on the business requirement. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/kms/docs/separation-of-duties Additional Information: Users granted with Owner (roles/owner) and Editor (roles/editor) have privileges equivalent to Cloud KMS Admin and Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter/Decrypter. To avoid misuse, Owner and Editor roles should be granted to a very limited group of users. Use of these primitive privileges should be minimal.
input_row.impact: Removed roles should be assigned to another user based on business needs.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/kms/docs/separation-of-duties Additional Information: Users granted with Owner (roles/owner) and Editor (roles/editor) have privileges equivalent to Cloud KMS Admin and Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter/Decrypter. To avoid misuse, Owner and Editor roles should be granted to a very limited group of users. Use of these primitive privileges should be minimal.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The control can be verified using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands, focusing on IAM roles assigned to users. This involves checking the separation of duties regarding the roles associated with Cloud KMS. Since IAM roles and their assignments are standard properties that can be queried through GCP APIs and there are explicit instructions in the audit field that describe how the verification can be performed using the `gcloud` command, the audit approach is determined to be Automated.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_kms_iam_role_separation_enforced

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **GCP API calls/Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) SDK methods to use:** Use the `gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID` command to retrieve the IAM policy of the specified project, which includes all users and their role assignments.
  
- **GCP CLI commands (if applicable):** 
  - Execute `gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID` to get the IAM policy details for the project.
  
- **Specific resources to enumerate:** IAM roles and their assignments within the project, focusing on roles related to Cloud KMS.
  
- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:** 
  - Ensure no user simultaneously has the `roles/cloudkms.admin` and any of the following roles:
    - `roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyEncrypterDecrypter`
    - `roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyEncrypter`
    - `roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyDecrypter`
  
- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** No users have both the `roles/cloudkms.admin` and any of the `Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter/Decrypter`, `Cloud KMS CryptoKey Encrypter`, or `Cloud KMS CryptoKey Decrypter` roles assigned.
  - **Fail:** At least one user has both the `roles/cloudkms.admin` and one or more of the specified `Cloud KMS` roles assigned.

This automated procedure will effectively ensure compliance with the control by verifying role assignments in a project to enforce the separation of duties in line with CIS recommendations.
